title: Missing Server-Side Authorization
description: The endpoint returns order data without verifying that the authenticated user owns the requested object.
impact: An authenticated attacker can access another customer's order information.
recommendation: Apply object-level authorization to every request and deny access by default.
observation: The API accepted a tampered identifier and returned a different customer's order details.
risk: Sensitive customer data is exposed to any authenticated user.

title: Verbose Error Messages
description: Unhandled errors expose internal implementation details to the client.
impact: Attackers can use the leaked details to improve later attacks.
recommendation: Return generic client-facing errors and log detailed diagnostics only on the server.
observation: Stack traces and SQL fragments were visible in the response body during error handling.
risk: Implementation details can be used for targeted exploitation.

title: Missing Audit Logging
description: Sensitive administrative actions are not recorded consistently.
impact: Security and administrative changes cannot be reliably attributed.
recommendation: Record immutable audit events for all privileged actions.
observation: Account changes completed successfully without any durable audit trail.
risk: Lack of traceability reduces incident response confidence.

title: Weak Session Timeout Enforcement
description: Long-lived sessions remain valid well beyond the documented timeout window.
impact: A stolen session can be reused for a longer period than expected.
recommendation: Enforce idle and absolute expiry on all session tokens.
observation: An inactive session stayed usable after the timeout threshold had elapsed.
risk: Session hijacking exposure increases with the extended lifetime.
